dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal calls for longer-term trend-following on 50-period timeframe with regime shifts and abnormal price moves. Implemented using SMA crossover (50/200) as the primary trend-following mechanism. ATR > 1.5% (scaled to decimal) proxies for abnormal price volatility (>1.5 std devs). Entry requires both trend confirmation and volatility spike. Exit triggers on trend reversal. Context-dependent direction reflects both LONG and SHORT applicability based on regime shift direction.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal calls for longer-term trend-following on 50-period timeframe with regime shifts and abnormal price moves. Implemented using SMA crossover (50/200) as the primary trend-following mechanism. ATR > 1.5% (scaled to decimal) proxies for abnormal price volatility (>1.5 std devs). Entry requires both trend confirmation and volatility spike. Exit triggers on trend reversal. Context-dependent direction reflects both LONG and SHORT applicability based on regime shift direction.

source_rule_text: A trader should use longer-term trend-following indicators to reduce transaction costs and capture abnormally large price moves that occur during major market shifts, rather than relying on shorter-term indicators that generate more frequent signals.
